nodes.text: The house settles after the storm. Dorothy and Toto step into bright fields and tiny cottages.

choices.label: Take a careful look before moving
Greet the nearby townspeople kindly

nodes.text: The Good Witch of the North explains that Dorothy has landed in a strange country and gives guidance for the road ahead.

choices.label: Ask how to reach home
Listen to every warning

nodes.text: Dorothy receives the silver slippers and a protective kiss, then turns toward the distant city.

choices.label: Begin the road at once

nodes.text: A Scarecrow on a pole waves his painted sleeve and asks to be set down.

choices.label: Free him quickly
Study the knots first

nodes.text: The Scarecrow asks to travel with Dorothy in hopes of gaining brains.

choices.label: Invite him to join the road

companionAction.scarecrow: Joined the road with a hopeful grin.

nodes.text: The companions follow the road through fields and orchards, arguing with stubborn trees and hunting clear waymarks.

choices.label: Let Scarecrow pick the best route
Push onward with pace

nodes.text: In a damp wood the travelers find a Tin Woodman standing motionless, rusted at every joint.

choices.label: Find the oil can and begin carefully
Check each hinge before turning valves

nodes.text: Freed from rust, the Tin Woodman says he seeks a heart and asks to join the company.

choices.label: Welcome him to the party

companionAction.tin-woodman: Promised to guard the road with a bright axe.

nodes.text: A Lion leaps from the trees with a roar, then admits his courage fails him.

choices.label: Speak firmly and invite honesty
Speak gently and lower the tension

nodes.text: The Lion asks to travel to the Emerald City and seek true courage.

choices.label: Accept him as a companion

companionAction.cowardly-lion: Joined with a shy promise to stand firm.

nodes.text: Roots and low branches force careful movement while the trees grow denser.

choices.label: Let each companion call hazards
Cross quickly before dusk

nodes.text: From the deep wood come Kalidahs, fierce beasts with bodies like bears and heads like tigers, driving the company toward a chasm.

choices.label: Race over the fallen tree before they close in
Study the beasts and track their movement

nodes.text: The tree trunk shakes behind them as the Kalidahs leap. A careful choice now could keep the path open.

choices.label: Have the Tin Woodman chop the far end of the trunk

companionAction.tin-woodman: Dropped the trunk at exactly the right moment.

choices.label: Use Scarecrow's route plan to draw the beasts onto the weakest span
choices.companionBonusTag: Companion Bonus Applied: Scarecrow found the weak span.

companionAction.scarecrow: Marked where the trunk would break cleanly.

nodes.text: A broad field of scarlet poppies lies across the road. Their heavy scent makes Dorothy drowsy while Scarecrow and the Tin Woodman keep moving.

choices.label: Press on quickly and carry Dorothy if needed
Seek help before everyone loses strength

nodes.text: Dorothy can no longer stay awake, and even the Lion sinks into sleep among the blossoms.

choices.label: Let the Tin Woodman and Scarecrow carry Dorothy while Toto scouts

companionBonus.tag: Companion Bonus Applied: Tin rescue efficiency +1.

companionAction.tin-woodman: Carried Dorothy without tiring through the flowers.
companionAction.scarecrow: Stayed clear-minded and led the route.

choices.label: Keep a close watch on the Lion and try to rouse him as long as possible

companionBonus.tag: Companion Bonus Applied: Lion courage reduced sleep penalty.

companionAction.cowardly-lion: Fought sleep for several more strides.

nodes.text: The Queen of the Field Mice arrives with her people and offers aid after Dorothy's party spares her little nation.

choices.label: Thank the Queen and organize a rescue line
Have Scarecrow coordinate teams by distance

nodes.text: Hundreds of mice pull together to free Dorothy from danger and set workers around the sleeping Lion.

choices.label: Guide the final pull and clear the road

nodes.text: At last the city glimmers ahead. The road remains long, but the companions press forward together.

choices.label: Close Chapter Pack #3

chapters.title: The Road to the Emerald City

nodes.text: Past the poppy fields the road grows smooth and wide. The friends pass tidy farms and green-painted houses as the city walls rise in the distance.

choices.label: Take in every sign of the city ahead
Ask Scarecrow to mark the approach route

companionAction.scarecrow: Counted every turn as the city walls came near.

nodes.text: A green-clad farmer points them to the great gate and warns that all visitors must obey the city's entry custom.

choices.label: Continue to the gate and present your request

nodes.text: At the high gate, a green-bearded Guardian asks your business and listens while each traveler names a wish for the Wizard.

choices.label: State each companion's request plainly

companionAction.scarecrow: Declared his need for brains without wavering.
companionAction.tin-woodman: Spoke quietly of his wish for a heart.
companionAction.cowardly-lion: Admitted his fear and asked for courage.

choices.label: Let Dorothy ask first to be sent home

nodes.text: The Guardian explains that all in the city wear green spectacles, fastened and locked, because the city's brightness would otherwise dazzle the eyes.

choices.label: Accept the admission protocol

nodes.text: With straps checked and clasps sealed, the green spectacles are fixed in place. The companions pass through the gate and see every street washed in brilliant green light.

choices.label: Step inside and keep close together

nodes.text: Servants direct the company toward rooms and send word ahead. Every companion repeats the reason for coming: brains, heart, courage, and Dorothy's journey home.

choices.label: Prepare for city errands before seeing the Wizard

chapters.title: A Short Turn Through the City

nodes.text: A brief halt inside the walls offers three choices: rest at an inn, walk the shining streets, or go straight toward the palace district.

choices.label: Choose the next stop before the palace
Ask the Lion to keep spirits up on the crowded streets
choices.companionBonusTag: Companion Bonus Applied: Lion steadied the group through the crowd.

companionAction.cowardly-lion: Walked at the front despite trembling paws.

nodes.text: At the palace approach, attendants record each petition and instruct the travelers to wait for an appointment with the great Wizard.

choices.label: Wait for the summons and keep the party ready
Have the Tin Woodman reassure Dorothy while they wait
choices.companionBonusTag: Companion Bonus Applied: Tin Woodman kept everyone calm while waiting.

companionAction.tin-woodman: Spoke kindly while the appointment was arranged.

nodes.text: The party is told their first audience will be granted in order. Dorothy and her friends gather themselves for what comes next.

choices.label: End Pack #4 and await the audience

nodes.text: Servants in green guide the party to a quiet waiting hall. Names and requests are written in order before anyone may enter the audience chamber.

choices.label: Keep the group settled until called

nodes.text: Dorothy enters first and sees a mighty head set high in the chamber, solemn and watchful. She asks to be sent home to Kansas.

choices.label: Speak plainly and ask for home

nodes.text: Scarecrow later reports a shining lady upon a seat of light. He states his wish for brains and listens for the Wizard's terms.

choices.label: Let Scarecrow ask directly
Have Scarecrow ask how the promise will be kept
choices.companionBonusTag: Companion Bonus Applied: Scarecrow asked a sharper question.

companionAction.scarecrow: Pressed for clear terms before bowing.

nodes.text: Tin Woodman says he faced a fearsome beast in the same chamber and still spoke for a heart. He keeps his voice gentle despite the sight.

choices.label: Continue with the formal request
Have Tin answer with kindness before asking
choices.companionBonusTag: Companion Bonus Applied: Tin Woodman answered with empathy.

companionAction.tin-woodman: Spoke with courtesy even before his own plea.

nodes.text: The Lion describes a burning sphere that seemed ready to consume him. He trembles, then asks for courage all the same.

choices.label: Let the Lion finish his request
Ask the Lion to steady his breath first
choices.companionBonusTag: Companion Bonus Applied: Lion steadied himself before speaking.

companionAction.cowardly-lion: Held his voice firm through the final words.

nodes.text: After hearing all four petitions, the Wizard gives one answer: the party must first settle the matter of the Witch of the West.

choices.label: Record the four requests and the condition

chapters.title: A Promise Before the Westward Road

nodes.text: Outside the audience hall, Dorothy and her companions compare what each one saw. Though the forms differed, the command was the same: go west before any wish is granted.

choices.label: Agree to travel west together

nodes.text: The four petitions are written on a new quest card: home, brains, heart, and courage. The party binds itself to a westward journey at dawn.

choices.label: Take the quest card and set the westward objective

nodes.text: At dawn the party studies the quest card and leaves the Emerald City by the western gate. Dorothy keeps the silver slippers close as they follow the road into unknown country.

choices.label: Set the westward line and keep moving

nodes.text: On the march, each companion repeats a simple aim: Dorothy for Kansas, Scarecrow for brains, Tin Woodman for a heart, and Lion for courage. They agree to hold one pace until the west is crossed.

choices.label: Keep a tight traveling order

nodes.text: The road enters the western lands where the ground runs yellow and broken into long hills. The wind is dry, and the party sees few homes along the ridges.

choices.label: Enter the Country of the Winkies

nodes.text: The travelers notice watch-fires on far rises and old warning marks cut into stone. No attack comes yet, but someone in the west has measured their road.

choices.label: Press on before night closes

nodes.text: A hollow between stone banks offers brief cover. Scattered marks show where western scouts once signaled across the hills, and the party slips through unseen.

choices.label: Rejoin the main road with new notes

nodes.text: From her western stronghold, the Witch of the West watches the road with her far-seeing eye and learns Dorothy carries the silver slippers. Soon an ink-dark sweep moves over ridges ahead of the party.

choices.label: Move between cover and avoid the sweep

nodes.text: The shadow passes, then returns from another angle. Scarecrow marks safer hollows while Tin and Lion guard Dorothy and Toto through the narrowing paths.

choices.label: Follow Scarecrow's quick route marks

companionAction.scarecrow: Mapped cover breaks under the western sweep.

nodes.text: Before sunset they glimpse workers driven under harsh command near the western heights, and word spreads that the Witch binds Winkies to forced labor. Beyond the ridge rises her high dwelling in the west.

choices.label: Take cover and watch the lines of labor

nodes.text: Night signals answer each other across the hills. The companions prepare for seizure and vow that, if one is taken, the others will attempt a rescue at once.

choices.label: Hold formation and prepare for capture resistance

nodes.text: The western watch closes quickly, and Dorothy with Toto is seized and taken toward the Witch of the West's hold. Dorothy keeps steady and marks turns in the road for any companion who might follow.

choices.label: Stay calm and remember each gate

nodes.text: Inside the western hold, Dorothy is set to tasks among Winkie servants while Toto keeps close. She waits for the right moment instead of wasting strength in panic.

choices.label: Watch the routines and wait for an opening

nodes.text: Dorothy gains the Golden Cap and learns its command over the Winged Monkeys. The charm allows three commands, and each use must be chosen carefully.

choices.label: Claim the cap and record the three commands

nodes.text: Dorothy keeps the cap hidden and plans how each command might serve rescue, travel, and escape if companions reach the hold.

choices.label: Save the first command for rescue

nodes.text: The hold keeps Dorothy on short daily chores under guard while Winkie servants move through the yards. She chooses between conserving strength or risking a small escape probe.

choices.label: Conserve strength and gather routine details
Test one route and retreat before alarm

nodes.text: By night, Dorothy and Toto keep low and pass signs with friendly Winkies who whisper that her companions are searching nearby.

choices.label: Prepare a silent signal for allies

nodes.text: Scarecrow, Tin Woodman, and the Lion reach the outer lines and try to draw Dorothy clear. The hold closes fast, leaving only seconds to choose a command.

choices.label: Use the cap command: Aid Rescue
Signal companions and run without a command

nodes.text: The party reaches an inner latch that opens only after gathering route tokens. A second command can carry the companions over a collapsing span if needed.

choices.label: Use the cap command: Carry Companions
Cross by handholds and keep the line tight

companionAction.scarecrow: Mapped handholds through the hold wall.
companionAction.tin-woodman: Lifted a gate arm for Dorothy and Toto.
companionAction.cowardly-lion: Held patrols at bay while the others crossed.

nodes.text: Dorothy and companions clear the hold and regroup with Winkie allies. They swear to finish the western danger and then resume the road toward home.

choices.label: Set the vow and prepare to return at dawn

nodes.text: In the western hold, pressure rises again as the Witch of the West closes on Dorothy. Scarecrow, Tin Woodman, and the Lion regroup nearby, ready to shield her at the first opening.

choices.label: Keep formation and wait for a clean moment

nodes.text: Dorothy spots water close at hand and prepares to act when the shadow swell peaks. The companions hold lanes and buy her the timing she needs.

choices.label: Ready the water and commit to the timing

nodes.text: Dorothy casts water at the right instant, and the western danger breaks at once. The threat that held the hold together falls away.

choices.label: Hold steady and finish the action

nodes.text: Dorothy and Toto step back in surprise, then call to the others. The hold is no longer under the same command, and the rescue line can move freely.

choices.label: Regroup with companions and check every gate

nodes.text: Freed Winkies gather in the courts and thank Dorothy and her companions. They offer lanterns, food, and guides for the road back east.

choices.label: Accept help and listen to local road advice

nodes.text: Winkie workers confirm the roads are safer and ask the travelers to remember them kindly when they reach the city again.

choices.label: Promise to carry their thanks eastward

nodes.text: Dorothy checks the Golden Cap and counts how many commands remain. The number is recorded before they leave the western hold behind.

choices.label: Record remaining commands and keep the cap secure

nodes.text: A Winkie elder notes that the Winged Monkeys still answer lawful cap calls. Dorothy saves any remaining command for the road ahead.

choices.label: Save remaining commands for necessary travel

nodes.text: With the western danger ended, Dorothy sets the next objective: return to the Emerald City and speak with the Wizard again.

choices.label: Set the return objective and gather supplies

nodes.text: The party turns from the western hold toward eastern roads, with Winkie guides pointing the first miles and Dorothy keeping the silver slippers ready.

choices.label: Begin the return road at first light

nodes.text: Dorothy and her companions return to the Emerald City and are received under the same city customs as before. The green spectacles remain part of every formal approach within the walls.

choices.label: Enter the city and report the western mission complete

nodes.text: Officials guide the party to a private audience where the Wizard must answer the promises made before the westward journey.

choices.label: Present each request clearly and wait for his answer

nodes.text: Dorothy and companions discover the Wizard is an ordinary man using devices and stagecraft to preserve authority. He admits the deception and asks to mend what he can.

choices.label: Hear the full account and keep the meeting calm

nodes.text: Though ordinary, the Wizard promises to honor his word in symbolic form for Scarecrow, Tin Woodman, and the Lion before attempting Dorothy's return.

choices.label: Proceed to the companion gifts

nodes.text: The Wizard presents Scarecrow with a symbolic brains token, honoring his thoughtful leadership on the road.

choices.label: Record Scarecrow's gift

nodes.text: Tin Woodman receives a symbolic heart token, a sign of the care he already showed through every trial.

choices.label: Record Tin Woodman's gift

nodes.text: The Lion receives a symbolic courage token, confirming the bravery he proved despite fear.

choices.label: Record the Lion's gift and move to departure

nodes.text: The Wizard prepares a balloon ascent for Dorothy's return. Ropes, basket, and wind checks are rushed into place while the party holds the yard clear.

choices.label: Assist launch preparation carefully

nodes.text: The balloon lifts with the Wizard aboard, but Dorothy is delayed by a final scramble and cannot reach the basket in time.

choices.label: Watch the ascent and hold steady for what comes next

nodes.text: Dorothy remains in Oz after the failed departure and gathers with her companions to decide a new path home.

choices.label: Set a new objective and begin planning immediately
